page: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 1952.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 30.5 GB of memory per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes.

In benchmark testing, the m3-ultramem-64 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core tests. It also achieves strong results in Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance is average, as shown by Geekbench and stress-ng single-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 3306.5 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 61.86 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring massive RAM footprints without the need for proportional scaling of vCPU counts. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, limiting its suitability for tasks requiring high-speed local scratch space or hardware-accelerated graphics processing.

The m3-ultramem-64 is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-heavy data analytics. It is also capable of handling moderate CPU-based LLM inference, as evidenced by its strong performance in text generation benchmarks for medium-sized models.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 is a memory-optimized server configured with 64 vCPUs and 1952.0 GB of RAM, delivering 30.5 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz, this instance features 32 physical cores with hyper-threading. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. In benchmark evaluations, the instance shows strong multi-core CPU performance, Passmark database operations, and static web serving throughput, while single-core performance remains average. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require proportional compute scaling. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, memory-intensive data processing, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs and 1952 GB RAM. It features strong multi-core performance and is designed for large-scale in-memory databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-ultramem-64 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 1952 GB RAM, and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring 1952 GB of RAM and 64 vCPUs for demanding in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 1952.0 GB of RAM
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and heavy data analytics
